FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Year 3 (2019-2021), N=10,054 early adolescents ages 12-13

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; ordinal logistic regression models assessing associations between parenting practices and media consumption outcomes, adjusted for covariates

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in BMC Pediatrics

FT_IC2.evidence: Paper examines associations between media parenting practices and R-rated movie consumption and mature-rated video game play. Outcomes are content-rating variables (R-rated, mature-rated), not smartphone/SM use or time. Per v6 criteria: 'Content-rating variables (mature-rated games, R-rated movies) alone do NOT qualify; rating ≠ modality.' No qualifying SM/phone variable as IV or DV.

notes: Content rating alone does not meet FT-IC2 qualifying exposure definition

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, baseline 2016-2018 (ages 9-10) to Year 3 2019-2021 (ages 12-13), N=9,519

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; longitudinal compositional data analysis (CoDA) with mixed-effects modeling examining changes in 24-hour movement behaviors across four annual assessments

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in BMC Pediatrics

FT_IC2.evidence: Paper examines descriptive longitudinal trends in screen time, sleep, and sports/exercise in 24-hour time-use composition. No analysis pairs a qualifying SM/phone variable as IV or DV with an individual-trait-level outcome variable (neural, behavioral, health, cognitive, well-being, etc.). Screen time is treated as a behavioral outcome/composition element, not as an exposure associated with a trait-level outcome. No inferential SM/phone ↔ trait relationship.

notes: Descriptive time-use study; no SM/phone exposure paired with individual-trait outcome

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Years 2, 3, 4 follow-up data, N=2,749, mean age 11.9 at Year 2

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; causal mediation analysis examining mediating effect of caffeine use on relationship between gaming and sleep

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in Journal of Behavioral Addictions

FT_IC2.evidence: Paper examines 'gaming duration' and 'problematic gaming' without isolating phone-specific or SM-specific gaming. Per v6 criteria: 'TV-only · console/PC-gaming-only · unspecified-platform-gaming-only · generic internet use without device specificity' do NOT qualify. Gaming modality is unspecified (could be console, PC, unspecified platform); not explicitly phone or SM-based. Does not meet qualifying exposure definition.

notes: Gaming is unspecified platform; no explicit phone or SM-based gaming measurement
